content.full_text:
July 29, 2005

Gerald James
Professional Engineers In Local Government
660 J Street, Suite 445
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. 05-149

Dear Mr. James:

We are in receipt of your July 21, 2005, letter requesting assistance under the provisions of the Political Reform Act.¹ The Fair Political Practices Commission may only provide formal written advice to someone whose duties are in question under the Act or to their authorized representation. (Government Code Section 83114(b).) Regulation 18329(b)(2) specifies:

"(2) Requests for formal written advice will not be acted upon unless the following requirements are met:
(A) The name, title or position, and mailing address of the person whose duties are in question are provided. In addition, if the request is submitted by an authorized representative, it shall contain a specific statement that such authorization has been made."

Since this letter is hypothetical in nature, regulation 18329(b)(8)(D), states that this is a circumstance in which the FPPC may decline to provide written advice. I have enclosed a copy of regulation 18329 for your review.

¹ Government Code sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

Please contact me at (916) 322-5660 should you have any additional questions.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel

Enclosure
JWW:rd
